summary: This cluster focuses on the taxonomic and phylogenetic characterization of red algae, specifically within the family Delesseriaceae, using molecular and morphological methods. A key finding is the discovery and description of *Erythroglossum hyacinthinum*, a new species from deep waters off the Korean Peninsula, distinguished by its unique blade morphology and *rbcL* gene sequences. The research underscores the utility of DNA barcoding in resolving phylogenetic relationships and identifying cryptic diversity within the genus *Erythroglossum*. Geographically, the work centers on the temperate Northwest Pacific, expanding knowledge of regional macroalgal biodiversity.
